capabilities.description: Natural language queries return contextually relevant document chunks based on meaning, not keywords

example.query: What materials are restricted in fine fragrance at concentrations above 0.1%?
example.retrieval_method: Dense embedding search → Rerank → Top-10 retrieval

capabilities.description: Combine semantic and lexical search for high-recall retrieval with precise keyword matching (CAS numbers, regulation IDs)

example.retrieval_method: RRF fusion of dense (semantic) + BM25 (keyword) results

capabilities.description: Every generated answer includes source citations with document ID, page number, and relevant excerpt

format.footer: Full reference list with hyperlinks to source documents

capabilities.description: Maintain conversation context across sessions; user can refer to previous queries and the system remembers domain context
capabilities.implementation: Vector store of conversation embeddings with time-decay weighting (exponential decay, half-life=7 days)

capabilities.description: Multi-turn conversations that maintain context, allow follow-up refinement, and handle clarification questions
Generate executive summaries of long documents (IFRA amendments, market reports, research papers)
Answer specific questions about fragrance formulation, compliance, materials, and market intelligence with sourced answers

sample_interactions.question: Suggest alternatives to Eugenol that maintain the spicy-clove character
sample_interactions.answer:
Based on molecular similarity (GNN embedding) and sensory panel data, the top alternatives to Eugenol (CAS 97-53-0) are:

1. **Isoeugenol (CAS 97-54-1)** — 0.94 molecular similarity. Slightly sweeter, more carnation-like. Cost: $18/kg vs. Eugenol $12/kg. [1] IFRA restriction: 0.02% in Category 4 (fine fragrance) due to sensitization risk.

2. **Dihydroeugenol (CAS 2785-87-7)** — 0.89 similarity. Milder, less pungent. $22/kg. [1] IFRA: 0.5% in Category 4. Better safety profile than isoeugenol.

3. **Methyl Diantilis (Givaudan, CAS 5595-61-3)** — 0.81 similarity in olfactory space. Carnation-spice with vanilla undertone. $45/kg. [2] Supplier: Givaudan. IFRA: unrestricted.

**Recommendation**: For fine fragrance at 0.5% concentration, Dihydroeugenol provides the best safety-to-character ratio. For cost-sensitive applications, retain Eugenol but include 0.05% BHT as antioxidant stabilizer.

citations.excerpt: Isoeugenol: QRA Category 4 limit 0.02%. Dihydroeugenol: QRA Category 4 limit 0.5%.
Methyl Diantilis® — spicy carnation specialty with vanilla-musk drydown. IFRA unrestricted.

sample_interactions.question: Compare Formula A (FRM-2104, Nocturne Noir) and Formula B (FRM-0891, Bois d'Orient)
sample_interactions.answer:
**Comparative Analysis: Nocturne Noir vs. Bois d'Orient**

| Dimension | Nocturne Noir (FRM-2104) | Bois d'Orient (FRM-0891) |
|-----------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Olfactory Family | Woody Oriental | Woody Oriental |
| Overall Similarity | — | 0.874 (GNN cosine) |
| Cost/kg | $112.70 | $98.40 |
| Hedonic Score | 78.4 | 81.2 |
| IFRA Compliance | Full (51st Amd.) | Full (51st Amd.) |
| Key Differentiator | Rose-centric with sandalwood | Amber-centric with vanilla |
| Market Position | Luxury EDP, $180/100ml | Luxury EDP, $145/100ml |

**Shared DNA**: Both use Hedione (18.2% vs 16.8%) as the dominant transparent floralizer, Sandalwood Oil, Vanillin, and Patchouli in similar proportions. The core woody-oriental skeleton is nearly identical.

**Divergence**: Nocturne Noir builds on Rose Absolute (8.0%) as the hero ingredient with cost implications ($45/kg). Bois d'Orient achieves its amber character through a Labdanum-Ambermax-Coumarin accord at half the material cost. The rose-to-amber shift is the primary differentiator — Nocturne Noir targets the feminine-leaning market while Bois d'Orient is unisex-positioned.

**Recommendation**: For a flanker line at a lower price point ($120/100ml), adopt Bois d'Orient's amber dry-down while retaining Nocturne Noir's rose top-note signature. Estimated cost: $91/kg (19.3% savings).

citations.source: NPD Group Prestige Fragrance Q2 2026, p.28
citations.excerpt: Woody Oriental segment growing 12% YoY; rose-centric variants +18%

sample_interactions.question: Find ingredients below 20 USD/kg suitable for a luxury masculine fougère
sample_interactions.answer:
Based on the VITO material catalog limited to fragrance-grade materials below $20.00/kg suitable for fougère compositions:

| Material | CAS | Cost/kg | Supplier | IFRA Cat 4 | Olfactory Role |
|----------|-----|---------|----------|-----------|----------------|
| Lavandin Grosso Oil | 8022-15-9 | $14.20 | Biolandes | Unrestricted | Herbaceous top |
| Bergamot Oil BF | 8007-75-8 | $18.50 | Capua | 0.4% bergapten | Citrus top |
| Coumarin | 91-64-5 | $8.30 | Symrise | 1.6% | Hay-tonka base |
| Iso E Super | 54464-57-2 | $12.40 | IFF | Unrestricted | Woody-amber |
| Hedione | 24851-98-7 | $16.80 | Firmenich | Unrestricted | Jasmine-transparent |
| Ambroxan | 6790-58-5 | $18.90 | Firmenich | Unrestricted | Ambery-marine |
| Linalool Synthetic | 78-70-6 | $7.50 | BASF | Unrestricted | Floral-fresh |
| Geranyl Acetate | 105-87-3 | $11.20 | IFF | Unrestricted | Rose-geranium |
| Galaxolide 50% | 1222-05-5 | $9.40 | IFF | Unrestricted | Clean musk |
| Cedarwood Virginia Oil | 8000-27-9 | $12.80 | Albert Vieille | Unrestricted | Woody-pencil |

**52 total materials matched. Full catalog with filtering options available in Materials → Pricing.**

citations.source: Materials Catalog, filtered: cost_per_kg <= 20.00, olfactory_family IN (aromatic, fougère, woody, citrus, herbal)

citations.excerpt: Category 4 (Fine Fragrance) concentration limits verified for all materials

implementation.pruning: LRU with minimum relevance score threshold of 0.3

capabilities: Recall previous questions about the same formula/project
Understand when user refers to 'that ingredient from earlier'
Remember user preferences (preferred suppliers, target markets, cost constraints)
Persist across browser sessions (encrypted local storage + server-side sync)
